# Fiberlytic Core Workflow — Developer Handoff Package

**Prepared for**: rebuilding or integrating the core business loop into another application.
**Scope**: everything *around* the Field Map — Project setup, Rate Cards, how a Field Map billing line becomes a QA/QC review item, how that becomes a Production entry, how that becomes P&L, and how the Dashboard rolls all of it up. Companion to `docs/FIELD_MAP_HANDOFF.md`, which covers the Field Map module itself (drawing, Work Objects, photos, billing capture) in depth and is not repeated here.
**Status of this document**: generated by direct source-code inspection (file/line citations throughout) on 2026-07-16. Documentation-only — nothing in the running app was changed to produce it.

> **Read this first — two things that will surprise you if you assume otherwise:**
>
> 1. **Dashboard and DailyPnL do NOT share one rollup function.** Despite computing what looks like the same revenue/cost/profit picture, `Dashboard.tsx`'s admin view and `DailyPnL.tsx` each independently compute their own aggregates, with different labor-cost fallback logic. They agree today because both were built off the same underlying `PnLEntry`/QA data, but there is no single source-of-truth selector enforcing that. **When you rebuild this, use one shared rollup function** — don't replicate the duplication, it's an existing inconsistency risk in this codebase, not a design worth preserving.
> 2. **A Project has exactly one `rateCardId`.** Not one-per-division. A project doing both Underground and Aerial work bills off a single Rate Card whose own `divisions` array happens to cover both — there is no native multi-rate-card-per-project model except the Subcontractor-level per-project override described in §2.

---

## 1. The End-to-End Loop

```
Client  →  Rate Card (unit codes + prices)  →  Project (rateCardId: one card)
                                                      │
                                                      ▼
                                    Field Map: draw a Work Object, pick a
                                    rate code, set quantity  →  MarkupBilling
                                    (qaStatus: undefined until submitted)
                                                      │
                                       "Submit to Production"
                                                      ▼
                                  MarkupBilling.qaStatus = 'pending_review'
                                                      │
                                    admin/supervisor reviews at /qa-review
                                          │                    │
                                     approve                reject (note required)
                                          │                    │
                                          ▼                    ▼
                                     'approved'           'rejected' → crew/sub
                                          │                fixes → 'rejection_fixed'
                                          │                    │ (back to review queue)
                                          ▼                    ▼
                              ProductionEntry auto-created (sourceMarkupId set)
                                          │
                                          ▼
                              PnLEntry auto-created (revenue = qty × rate)
                                          │
                                          ▼
                        Dashboard / DailyPnL independently roll this up
```

Nothing here is one single pipeline function — it's several independent triggers (see each numbered section). A production entry can also be logged manually with no Field Map / QA involvement at all (§7).

---

## 2. Project Creation & Setup

- **No setup wizard.** `NewProjectModal` (`src/pages/Projects.tsx:277-394`) is one form; a separate `EditProjectModal` (`:396-527`) handles everything else, all post-creation.
- **Only `name` is validated** (`Projects.tsx:307`, non-empty). Everything else defaults silently: `status: 'planning'`, `startDate`/`dueDate` = today, `contractValue`/`budget`/`footageGoal` = 0, `retentionPct` = 10%, `crewIds: []`.
- **Full `Project` shape** (`src/types.ts:17-60`): `id`, `organizationId?`, `name`, `client` (free text), `clientId?` (FK→Client), `rateCardId?` (FK→RateCard — "the one rate card billing draws from," `types.ts:27`), `boundary?`, `location`, `status`, `workTypes[]`, `startDate`, `dueDate`, `contractValue`, `budget`, `footageGoal`, `footageComplete`, `crewIds[]`, `subcontractorIds?`, `supervisorId?`, `retentionPct?`, `notes?`.
- **Client association**: `ClientField` (`Projects.tsx:207-275`) — pick an existing `Client` or type a free-text name (which sets `client` but leaves `clientId` empty — no rate-card linkage possible for a free-text client). Picking a real client immediately surfaces that client's rate cards inline (`linkedCards`, `:225,252-268`) so `rateCardId` can be attached at creation, or skipped (defaults `null`).
- **Everything else happens on `ProjectDetail.tsx`**: rate card reassignment (admin-only `<select>`, `:226-254`), Supervisor Assignment (`:269-293`, filtered to `Employee.isSupervisor`), Crew & Subcontractor Assignment (`:295-380`, toggle chips), Project Files upload (`:382-480`), Job Site Boundary geofence draw (`:498-540`).

---

## 3. Rate Cards

**Hierarchy**: `Client` → `RateCard` → `RateCardUnit`.

- `Client` (`types.ts:273-285`): optional `divisions: RateCardDivision[]` (`'Underground' | 'Aerial'`) and a billing address used on invoice PDFs.
- `RateCard` (`types.ts:287-293`): `id, clientId, divisions: RateCardDivision[], name, effectiveDate`. **Client-scoped, not project-scoped**, and explicitly reusable — a client can have several cards (e.g. one per division). `RateCards.tsx` groups cards under each client with an "Add rate card" action per client (`:419-474`); the page's own description is literally "Client-specific unit pricing" (`:400`).
- `RateCardUnit` (`types.ts:295-304`): `id, rateCardId, unitCode, description, uom: 'LF'|'EA'|'SQFT', rate, category?` — one billable line item.
- **A Project attaches exactly one `RateCard`** via the single FK `Project.rateCardId` — set in the New/Edit Project modal's `ClientField`, or via `ProjectDetail.tsx:242-251`'s inline select.
- **The one exception**: `Subcontractor.projectRateCards?: {projectId, rateCardId}[]` (`types.ts:350-358`) lets a specific subcontractor bill against their own negotiated rate card for a given project, overriding the project's default. This is the only place a project effectively has more than one rate card active — keyed by which company is billing, not by division.

---

## 4. Rate Code Lookup — Two Different Pickers

Two independent implementations exist, scoped differently — worth knowing before you build one canonical version:

- **`AddWorkModal.tsx:130-133`** (the primary "Add Work" billing step): `effectiveRateCardId = subProjectRateCardId || project?.rateCardId`, then `rateCardUnits = data.rateCardUnits.filter(u => u.rateCardId === assignedRateCard.id)`. Strictly scoped to the **project's single assigned card** (or the subcontractor's override). UI is tabbed — `suggested` (matches `RateCardUnit.category` or the Work Type's `billingKeywords`, `:160-166`), `favorites` (`data.favoriteUnitCodes`), `recent`, `all`, plus text search. Dollar amounts are hidden for `field`/`supervisor` roles and shown as `rate × payRatePercent/100` for subcontractors (`:134-150`).
- **`MarkupPanel.tsx:202-214`**: a plain `<select>`, scoped more loosely — pools units from **every** rate card belonging to the project's client (not just `project.rateCardId`), falling back to all rate units app-wide if none match. Selecting a unit code auto-fills description/UOM/rate onto the `MarkupBilling` draft.
- **A `MarkupBilling` row has no live FK back to `RateCardUnit`** — `rateCode` plus a snapshotted `description`/`unitType`/`rate`/`total` (`types.ts:1025-1030`) are copied at save time. A later rate-card price change never retroactively touches an already-billed line — this is deliberate (billed work should reflect the price at the time it was billed), but it means there is no way to bulk-reprice historical billing lines from a rate-card edit.

---

## 5. Bulk Import

`BulkImportModal.tsx` ("Bulk Import Rate Card Units," `:391`) imports **only into `RateCardUnit`** — never into Projects or Clients. Flow: pick target Client + existing Rate Card (or create one inline) + Divisions, upload .xlsx/.xls/.csv, header-sniffed against alias lists for unit code/description/UOM/rate columns, per-row `new`/`update`/`error`/`warn` status shown before committing.

---

## 6. QA/QC Review State Machine

`MarkupBilling.qaStatus?: QaStatus` (`types.ts:1010,1042`) — 5 states: `pending_review | approved | rejected | rejection_fixed | approved_after_correction`. **`undefined` means "never submitted" and is always treated as pass-through** (never gated) everywhere this field is read — a load-bearing convention, not an edge case to special-case away.

**Transitions** (all in `src/store/DataContext.tsx`):

| From | To | Trigger | Who |
|---|---|---|---|
| `undefined` | `pending_review` | `submitMarkupToProduction` (`productionFromMarkup.ts:132`) | crew/field, subcontractor |
| `approved` (edited) | `pending_review` | `reopenQaIfAlreadyApproved` (`DataContext.tsx:802-828`) — editing quantity/rate on an already-approved line reopens it | anyone editing the line |
| `pending_review`/`rejection_fixed` | `approved` or `approved_after_correction` | `approveQaLine` (`:1855-1890`) — picks the "after correction" variant if the prior status was `rejection_fixed` | admin, supervisor |
| `pending_review`/`rejection_fixed` | `rejected` | `rejectQaLine` (`:1891-1924`) — **requires a non-empty note** | admin, supervisor |
| `rejected` | `rejection_fixed` | `markRejectionFixedQa` (`:1925-1955`) — the resubmit path; puts it back in the review queue, does **not** auto-approve | crew/field (`Dashboard.tsx:548`), subcontractor (`:1146`, actor prefixed `subcontractor:<id>`) |

**Role gating**: `/qa-review` is admin/supervisor only (`RoleGatedRoute`, `App.tsx:85`); a supervisor sees only rows where `project.supervisorId === activeSupervisorEmployeeId` (`QaReview.tsx:56-60`). `approveQaLine`/`rejectQaLine` are wired into `QaReview.tsx` and `MarkupPanel.tsx` only — both admin/supervisor surfaces.

**Row building**: `buildQaReviewRows` (`qaReview.ts:39-48`) — one row per `MarkupBilling` with a defined `qaStatus`, joined to its markup + project, soft-deleted markups excluded. `applyQaFilters` (`:109-124`) filters by project/client/employee/sub/date/qaStatus/approvedBy/reviewedBy, plus a collapsed 3-bucket `revenueStatus` derived from the 5 real statuses (`revenueStatusOf`, `:56-60`).

---

## 7. Production Entries

`ProductionEntry` (`types.ts:103-124`): `footage`, `hours`, `crewId` xor `subcontractorId`, and `sourceMarkupId` — set **only** when auto-generated from a Field Map submission.

**Two creation paths**, both funnel through `addProduction` (`DataContext.tsx:896`):
- **Automatic**: `submitMarkupToProduction` (`productionFromMarkup.ts:36-161`) groups approved-for-billing `MarkupBilling` lines by crew (one entry per crew, or one for a subcontractor) and calls `addProduction` with `sourceMarkupId` set, line items carrying `qaStatus: 'pending_review'`.
- **Manual**: `Production.tsx`'s "Log Crew Day" / "Rate Card Log" modal calls `addProduction` directly, no `sourceMarkupId`, no QA/Field Map involvement at all — this is a real, separate front door into Production, not a fallback.

`recomputeFootage` (`DataContext.tsx:765-772`) sums `entryDisplayFootage` (LF line-item total, falling back to raw `.footage`) across a project's production entries into `Project.footageComplete`, skipping already-`complete` projects. It runs on **every** write that touches production or its line items (add/edit/delete, QA-driven edits) — there's no scheduled recompute job; it's synchronous with every mutation.

---

## 8. P&L

`addProduction` (`DataContext.tsx:896-980`) **always** appends a `PnLEntry` (`:949-959`) — production and P&L are created together, one call, not two separate steps a caller has to remember.

- **Revenue** = sum of line items' `extendedTotal` if present, else `footage × rate`.
- **Cost**: `laborCost` = `payRatePercent` of revenue if `subcontractorId` is set; otherwise **`0`** for markup-sourced entries — real in-house labor cost flows separately, from Time Clock/timecards, not from this auto-generated entry (`DataContext.tsx:912-923`). `equipmentCost` is similarly `0` for markup-sourced entries. `materialCost`/`otherCost` are always `0` here regardless of source.
- `PnLEntry.productionEntryId` links back, for cascade-delete when the production entry is removed.

`src/lib/analytics.ts`: `pnlCost = labor+material+equipment+other` (`:64`); `pnlProfit = revenue - pnlCost` (`:65`); `summarizePnl` (`:68-75`) sums those over a `PnLEntry[]`; `dailyPnlSeries` (`:92-102`) buckets by date. **These two selectors are used only by `ProjectDetail.tsx:135` and `Projects.tsx:81`** — the Dashboard and DailyPnL pages do not use them (see §9).

---

## 9. Dashboard & DailyPnL Rollups

`AdminDashboard` (`Dashboard.tsx:1922`, dispatched by role at `:2702-2708`) does **not** call `summarizePnl`/`dailyPnlSeries`. It computes its own inline `summary`/`dailyData` aggregates (`:2023-2176`) with a bespoke labor-cost fallback chain — clock entries → timecards → PnL snapshot, plus subcontractor cost split — and retention-adjusted `netRevenue`/`ebitda`.

`DailyPnL.tsx` uses a *different* shared selector, `computeMetrics` (`analytics.ts:285`).

**So Dashboard and DailyPnL each independently roll up revenue/cost** — not off one shared function (see the callout at the top of this document). The one thing they *do* share: both import `computeAllProductionQaTotals`/`computeQaRevenueBreakdown` from `analytics.ts` for their Pending/Approved/Rejected QA cards (`Dashboard.tsx:2109-2112`, `DailyPnL.tsx:858`), which deep-link into `/qa-review` with pre-set filters.

---

## 10. Developer Integration Notes

If you're rebuilding this loop rather than porting the code verbatim, the minimum server-side surface is:

1. **Rate card lookup service** — given a project (+ optional subcontractor override), return the effective set of billable unit codes. Collapse the two picker implementations in §4 into one; don't carry the inconsistency forward.
2. **QA state machine** — implement §6's table as an actual state machine with role-gated transitions (`pending_review`/`rejection_fixed` → `approved`/`rejected` restricted to admin/supervisor; `rejected` → `rejection_fixed` restricted to the original submitter). Enforce "reject requires a note" server-side, not just in the UI.
3. **Production/P&L as one transaction** — §8's finding that they're always created together matters: don't split this into two independently-callable endpoints where a caller could create one without the other.
4. **One shared rollup function** for anything Dashboard-shaped, used by every page that shows revenue/cost/profit — this fixes the one known inconsistency in the current app rather than reproducing it.
5. **`workId` sequencing** — see the answer already given in conversation: current scheme is client-side read-then-increment, unsafe under concurrent multi-tenant writes; needs a real server-side sequence.
